author: D. Fox
theorem_statement: forall p, p.Prime -> digit_len p >= 13 -> symOf p = 1
classification: CONJECTURE (named open Prop C13_Law_Open; no theorem discharges it)
why_not_proven: Infinite/undecidable: the statement ranges over all primes; no finite computation settles it, and no general structural theorem (decode avoids every nontrivial W(H4) fixed locus) is established (and may be false for some large prime).

symOf is not kernel-checkable: kernel `decide` overflows maxRecDepth on a single symOf 191, so symOf p = 1 is unprovable by decide/rfl even for one prime; Modules A/B/C report symOf via #eval (compiled).
p.Prime and Nat.log10 require mathlib, which the mathlib-free direct-compile H4 line does not have (bringing in lake/mathlib is the documented destructive operation).
honest_note: C13_law is recorded as a NAMED OPEN conjecture (C13_Law_Open), NOT proven. boundary_proven is a Bool sample check over the explicit list, NOT a proof of the universal law. No sorry/admit/sorryAx/axiom used.
